---
title: "City2Graph · Build proximity graphs"
---

::: {.lead}
Build spatial proximity networks over Burjassot amenities: single-layer graphs
(KNN, Delaunay, radius…), multi-layer graphs that bridge feature types, and
group-node graphs that link zones to the points they contain.
:::

This walkthrough mirrors the
[Proximity Graphs panel](../../panels/city2graph/proximity.qmd).

## Before you begin

Create a point feature object — download amenities as points in
[Import urban data](data.qmd) (**Get as Points**), or convert footprints with
**Convert to Centroids**.

## 1. Single-layer graph

Expand **Single-Layer Graphs** and set:

- **Features** — the amenities object.
- **Graph Type** — start with **K-Nearest Neighbors** and **K** = `5`.
- **Distance Metric** — **Euclidean** (or **Network** with a street **Network**
  object).

Press **Generate Graph**.

![A KNN proximity graph over Burjassot amenities.](../../../assets/guides/city2graph/03-single-layer.png)

### Try other graph types

Switch the **Graph Type** to explore **Delaunay**, **Fixed Radius** (set
**Radius**), **Waxman** (set **Beta/R0/Seed**), **Gabriel**, **Relative
Neighborhood**, **EMST** or **Contiguity** (set **Queen/Rook**).

![The Graph Type dropdown with all topology options.](../../../assets/guides/city2graph/03-graph-types.png)

## 2. Multi-layer bridge graph

Expand **Multi-Layer Graphs**, assign **Layer 1** and **Layer 2** (e.g.
amenities and shops), pick a **Method** (**KNN** or **Fixed Radius**) and press
**Generate Multi-Layer Graph** to connect across layers.

![A multi-layer graph bridging two feature types.](../../../assets/guides/city2graph/03-multi-layer.png)

## 3. Group nodes (zones ▸ points)

Expand **Group Nodes**, set **Polygons** (zones) and **Points**, choose a
**Predicate** (**Covered By**, **Within**, **Intersects**) and press **Generate
Group Nodes** to link each point to its containing zone.

![Connecting points to the zones that contain them.](../../../assets/guides/city2graph/03-group-nodes.png)

## 4. Tune deduplication and visualization

In **Advanced Settings**, enable **Deduplicate Points** and set the **Dedup
Tolerance** when OSM ways imported as many vertices. In **Visualization**, adjust
**Edge Thickness** and the **Visualize Limit** for dense graphs.

![Advanced and visualization controls.](../../../assets/guides/city2graph/03-advanced.png)

## Next steps

Continue with [Analyze metapaths](metapaths.qmd) to connect amenities through the
street network.
